excluded: direct measurement of signed coherent RY offset on IBM hardware
a noisy-hardware robustness or auditability conclusion
supported: simulated signed coherent-offset sensitivity map on top of IBM backend-property stochastic noise
screening evidence for whether a cheaper coherent-offset calibration probe is worth designing
explicit non-submitting separation from the full 328-job IBM hardware run

next_gate: If a stable signed-offset region exists, design a small calibration probe for signed coherent bias before any full hardware run. If not, archive the current IBM hardware path or redesign the encoding target.
objective: Determine whether PhaseWrap's compact phase-wrap positional score has measurable robustness or auditability advantages on noisy quantum hardware, compared with matched positional-score encodings, under fixed circuit width.
